summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the key advantage of sexual reproduction compared to asexual reproduction?

Sexual reproduction combines genetic material from two parents through meiosis and fertilization, creating offspring with unique combinations of alleles. This genetic variation is the raw material for natural selection, allowing populations to adapt to new diseases, predators, and environmental changes. Asexual reproduction is faster but produces clones with no genetic diversity.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: What are the three stages of interphase, and what happens during each?

Interphase is the longest part of the cell cycle (~90% of time). G₁ (Gap 1): the cell grows, produces proteins, and carries out normal functions. S (Synthesis): DNA is replicated so each chromosome becomes two sister chromatids. G₂ (Gap 2): the cell continues to grow and synthesizes proteins needed for mitosis (e.g., tubulin for spindle fibers).

Example: What is the key advantage of sexual reproduction compared to asexual reproduction?

This genetic variation is the raw material for natural selection, allowing populations to adapt to new diseases, predators, and environmental changes. Asexual reproduction is faster but produces clones with no genetic diversity.
